Source: [here](https://sadashree.substack.com/p/odiya-culture).

Odiya culture

# Odiya culture

### Why is it so distinct

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Mar 18, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

One of the reasons is it’s geography itself, flanked by the Bay of the Bengal on one hand, and the Eastern Ghats on the other side, with the Chota Nagpur plateau in between. The state is a link between the Northern and Southern parts of India, thus inheriting certain common characteristics of both regions.Having passed, through different rulers, [Odisha](https://www.quora.com/What-makes-Odisha-history-and-culture-different-from-other-Indian-cultures-In-what-way-it-is-unique/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2), ended up experiencing various cultures. After Ashoka’s invasion, and the Kalinga War, the Buddhist influence was strong here, and that accounts for the large number of Buddhist sites. Similarly with the decline of Magadha, Kharavela assumed full power, and he promoted Jainism on large scale here. The latter Ganga and Gajapati dynasties, promoted Hinduism, in order to wean away people from Buddhism. Add to it elements of Tantric, Shakta tradition. So what you have in Odisha is a culture, that is predominantly a mix of Buddhist, Jaina, Vaishnava, Shaivite, Shakta traditions, with an influence of Tribal culture too.

The combination of sea, hills, forest, plateau, has given rise to a culture, that while being essentially Hindu in nature, has it’s own unique characteristics. It’s coastline, and a rich maritime tradition in ancient times, has seen something like the Bali Jatra commemorating it. It turns an entire town Bargarh into an open air theater, during Dhanu Jatra to enact the story of Krishna and Kansa. This was apparently a form of Thanksgiving by the farmers and has now become a regular feature of the State’s cultural calendar. Again while Odiya is the major language of the state, there are a whole lot of tribal dialects too here.

One of the major factors in the evolution of Odiya culture has been the tribal influence.The state has a large tribal population, with around 62 tribes, in around 13 categories scattered all over the city. This tribal influence is felt in every aspect of Odisha culture. The best example is the Jagannatha Ratha Yatra, where the Lord is believed to be in the form of Nila Madhava, a tribal deity worshiped in the hills. And it accounts for the rather peculiar shape of the idol, in a more animist form. Even it’s craftwork, and paintings have a lot of tribal influence, especially the Dhokra art work and paintings on the walls.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!st5g!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F29eb1272-a6a0-4322-ad38-6aec63a74d5c_400x512.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!st5g!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F29eb1272-a6a0-4322-ad38-6aec63a74d5c_400x512.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Again while Odissi is the state’s most famous dance form, known for it’s grace, it also has a lot of well known folk dances, representing it’s regional diversity.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!1ehP!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F239bc26b-d8b3-4838-81c2-a60e2f8329d9_602x399.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!1ehP!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F239bc26b-d8b3-4838-81c2-a60e2f8329d9_602x399.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Mayurbhanj Chau Dance, bears a distinct tribal influence.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!b-rf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F84e1772d-b68f-41e5-938d-e2432ba5bde4_602x339.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!b-rf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F84e1772d-b68f-41e5-938d-e2432ba5bde4_602x339.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Chaiti Ghoda dance more popular among the fishermen in coastal districts.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!mFO3!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb6f900e0-bae7-4494-8c31-a3b8be0cf8ab_210x315.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!mFO3!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb6f900e0-bae7-4494-8c31-a3b8be0cf8ab_210x315.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Mahari dance was more patronized by the Ganga rulers.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!IXgA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fef9b2b96-fc43-44f8-b223-b9f23089a7b2_602x707.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!IXgA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fef9b2b96-fc43-44f8-b223-b9f23089a7b2_602x707.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Gotipua dance, that narrates the Radha Krishna story, done mostly by young boys.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!smWf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F10e666b2-dba4-43b0-b49c-43fdf349aeae_602x993.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!smWf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F10e666b2-dba4-43b0-b49c-43fdf349aeae_602x993.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Again the Pattachitra paintings, were an outcome of the Jagannatha worship, with paintings being done on cloth.

If I have to summarize, it’s location between North and South, the presence of coast on one side, and hills on the other side, strong tribal influence, and intermingling of Jaina, Buddhist, Vaishnava, Jagannatha, Shaiva and Tantric traditions, has made Odiya culture have it’s own identity.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1
